items.title: Philippines orders arrest of fugitive senator wanted by ICC
items.summary: Justice Secretary says anyone helping Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa evade arrest will 'face consequences'.

items.title: ‘Homeland or death’: How Cuba would defend itself against a US attack
items.summary: A US military operation in Cuba could be imminent, but Havana is not entirely defenceless, some analysts argue.

items.title: Dropped Sardar Azmoun asserts his love for Iran, backs World Cup squad
items.summary: Forward wishes 'success and pride' to the Iranian team after being excluded from the squad for the 2026 tournament.

items.summary: What options do the US and Iran have left to bring war to an end?

items.title: Kite carrying Palestinian children’s messages reaches Mount Everest summit
items.summary: Mountaineers carry a kite bearing messages and signatures from children in Gaza to the top of the world.

items.title: Flooding in southern China washes away cars and destroys bridge
items.summary: Flooding in southern China washes away cars and destroys bridge

items.title: Ebola outbreak: When will a vaccine be developed for the new strain?
items.summary: The Bundibugyo strain of Ebola has a fatality rate of up to 50 percent and no approved vaccine as yet.

items.title: Iran reviews US proposal to end war as Pakistan steps up mediation efforts
items.summary: Pakistan military chief Asim Munir to travel to Tehran for talks, according to Iranian media reports.

items.title: ‘Iron brothers’: How China and Pakistan built an unlikely 75-year bond
items.summary: From secret nuclear exchanges to a key diplomatic deal, Pakistan and China have long helped each other. What's next?

items.title: World Cup 2026: Spain ex-players, fans cautiously embrace favourites tag
items.summary: Euro 2024 champions will feel the pressure of a football-mad nation as they aim to clinch World Cup-Euro double.

items.title: The economic shock of the Iran war will hit the world in four waves
items.summary: The impact of the conflict will not stop at energy price hikes. It will spread to various sectors and last years.

items.title: Mohamed Salah to captain Egypt as squad announced for FIFA World Cup 2026
items.summary: Versatile Nantes forward Mostafa Mohamed is a significant omission from the 27-man preliminary squad.

items.summary: Buddhist hall housing ‘eternal flame’ burns down in Japan

items.title: Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire, but its options may be limited
items.summary: Israeli officials and media indicate renewed conflict with Iran is being actively considered despite a shaky deadlock.

items.title: DR Congo cancel World Cup training camp in Kinshasa over Ebola outbreak
items.summary: DRC's public sendoff in the capital was also cancelled before their departure for the FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Taiwan’s President Lai says he would be happy to speak with Donald Trump
items.summary: US and Taiwan leaders have not spoken since Washington shifted diplomatic recognition from Taipei to Beijing in 1979.

items.title: ‘King’ Emery hailed as Aston Villa beat Freiburg 3-0 in Europa League
items.summary: Villa dominated Freiburg in the final in Istanbul to win their first trophy since 1996 and European title since 1982.

items.title: Walid Daqqa: A Parallel Life
items.summary: Walid Daqqa, the Palestinian prisoner who turned decades in Israeli jails into moments of thought, creativity and love.

items.title: The numbers behind global mental health and its different disorders
items.summary: Nearly one in eight people in the world lives with a mental disorder, and one person dies by suicide every 43 seconds.

items.title: Hebron’s Ibrahimi Mosque illuminated in Israeli flag colours
items.summary: Palestinian officials condemned Israel’s illumination of Hebron’s Ibrahimi Mosque with Israeli flag colours and slogans.

items.title: Iran war day 83: Tehran ‘reviewing’ latest US response to end conflict
items.summary: Iran says diplomacy with the US remains open as Hormuz tensions deepen and outrage grows over Israel’s flotilla arrests.

items.title: South Korea’s stock market soars as Samsung union calls off planned strike
items.summary: Benchmark KOSPI rises more than 8 percent following deal to avert strike at top memory chip maker.

items.title: MVP Gilgeous-Alexander stars as Thunder level series with Spurs
items.summary: Defending champs OKC win 122-113 as Gilgeous-Alexander scores 30 points and provides 9 assists, overshadowing Wembanyama.

items.title: UN adopts resolution supporting international court’s climate ruling
items.summary: Some 141 UN member states voted in support of the ICJ's finding climate change is an 'existential threat'.

items.title: Bolivia’s president reshuffles cabinet amid anti-government protests
items.summary: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz has announced a cabinet reshuffle and other measures as protests escalate.
